title: Out-of-Distribution Detection & Applications With Ablated Learned Temperature Energy
Detecting and Preventing Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models
authors: Anisha Gunjal, Jihan Yin, Erhan Bas

title: Enabling Calibration In The Zero-shot Inference Of Large Vision-Language Models
GlideNet: Global, Local and Intrinsic based Dense Embedding NETwork for Multi-category Attributes Prediction
CAR – Cityscapes Attributes Recognition: A Multi-category Attributes Dataset for Autonomous Vehicles
Natural Adversarial Objects
On The State of Data In Computer Vision: Human Annotations Remain Indispensable for Developing Deep Learning Models
Evaluating Deep Neural Networks Trained on Clinical Images in Dermatology with the Fitzpatrick 17k Dataset
A Survey of Deep Learning Approaches for OCR and Document Understanding
Generalization Analogies: A Testbed for Generalizing AI Oversight to Hard-To-Measure Domains
why: Concurrent work on reward-model/oversight generalization under distribution shift — the closest neighbor to this paper.

title: On Calibration of Modern Neural Networks
why: Foundational calibration / ECE + temperature scaling paper underpinning the calibration framing used across eval work.

title: Can You Trust Your Model's Uncertainty? Evaluating Predictive Uncertainty Under Dataset Shift
why: Foundational study of predictive uncertainty + calibration degradation under dataset shift (Ovadia et al., NeurIPS 2019) — the classification result this paper extends to reward models.

title: ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
why: AlexNet — the deep-CNN result on ImageNet that ignited modern vision; bridges the ImageNet and COCO nodes.

title: Rich Feature Hierarchies for Accurate Object Detection and Semantic Segmentation
why: R-CNN — the detector COCO expected to far outperform its DPM baseline; by a COCO co-author.

title: Object Detection with Discriminatively Trained Part Based Models
why: DPM — the actual baseline model COCO benchmarks (DPMv5) to show it is ~2x harder than PASCAL.

title: OverFeat: Integrated Recognition, Localization and Detection using Convolutional Networks
authors: Sermanet, Eigen, Zhang, Mathieu, Fergus, LeCun
why: Early end-to-end ConvNet detector COCO names as the kind of approach that should beat DPM.

title: Very Deep Convolutional Networks for Large-Scale Image Recognition
why: VGG -- the very deep 16/19-layer CNN; ILSVRC2014 localization winner and classification runner-up, and a backbone that became ubiquitous.

title: Going Deeper with Convolutions
why: GoogLeNet/Inception -- ILSVRC2014 classification winner (6.7% top-5) and the exact model benchmarked against humans in the ILSVRC paper.

title: Visualizing and Understanding Convolutional Networks
why: ZFNet -- Clarifai's ILSVRC2013 winner; deconvnet feature visualization that opened the CNN black box.

title: WordNet: A Lexical Database for English
why: The synset hierarchy ImageNet/ILSVRC classes hang on -- the semantic scaffold of the dataset.

title: mT5: A Massively Multilingual Pre-trained Text-to-Text Transformer
why: Canonical massively-multilingual encoder-decoder (101 languages); the multilingual-model lineage MGSM positions its few-shot prompting against.

title: Unsupervised Cross-lingual Representation Learning at Scale
why: XLM-R — the multilingual-encoder SOTA baseline MGSM beats on XCOPA/XL-WiC; a high-citation anchor for the multilingual cluster.
